label: Intent Routing Gateway
description: Determines whether the question should use document retrieval, live SQL, or both.
inputs: Processed result from: Workflow entry (user request or trigger)
outputs: Processed result from: Processed result from: Workflow entry (user request or trigger)
rationale: Auto-applied: catalog alternative identified during validation.

explanation.purpose: Classify each question as document-focused, database-focused, or mixed and send it to the right retrieval path.
explanation.role_in_workflow: After intake, this node decides how the question should be answered. It routes document-heavy questions to the enterprise knowledge-base path, KPI freshness questions to the live SQL path, and mixed questions to both so the system can produce one combined answer.
explanation.why_it_exists: The transcript explicitly confirmed a split design with support for both shared knowledge-base answers and live database lookups. Reusing the GraphRAG query agent here is sensible because the routing problem is mainly semantic classification over enterprise question types, and it avoids building a separate intent model from scratch.
explanation.chat_relationship: This directly implements the agreed design from the chat: intent routing should send KPI questions to SQL, document questions to retrieval, and mixed questions to both.
explanation.execution_summary: Determines whether the question should use document retrieval, live SQL, or both.
